summaries.summary: User greeted and asked if K_MIND can read Claude Desktop sessions. It cannot — Claude Code and Claude Desktop are isolated products with no shared session access.
User requested real-time conversation logging to filesystem. Chose JSON format for performance. Storage location: sessions/ folder.

User designed a 3-file memory architecture: far_memory (full conversation), near_memory (summaries with pointers), and mind_memory (mermaid mindmap as subconscious mind). Central node is 'knowledge'. mind_memory is the core file of the prototype.
User confirmed understanding: all 3 files (far_memory, near_memory, mind_memory) will be updated in real-time every turn — capturing both conversation and work results.
User asked about the meaning of 'constraints' in the mindmap. It represents known limitations/boundaries of the system — the mind knowing its own limits.

Major architecture evolution: 6 top-level mindmap groups (architecture, constraints, session, work, conventions, documentation). Auto-start via CLAUDE.md on session start, resume, and compaction recovery. Mind-first reading strategy — Claude reads mindmap first, digs into files only when needed.

Each top-level group gets its own folder with domain-specific JSON file. Session files are the dynamic brainstorming record, referencing accomplished work for concordance and continuity. Work is the stable anchor point.

Created /mind-context skill (.claude/skills/mind-context/SKILL.md). Default mode shows reduced mindmap (session, work, conventions, documentation only). /mind-context full shows complete mindmap. Outputs recent near_memory summaries after the mindmap. Updated CLAUDE.md lifecycle events to use /mind-context on start, resume, and compaction recovery.

Defined two conventions: (1) mindmap_display_layout — full mode top-down half/half (session,work,doc top; arch,constraints,conventions bottom), normal mode left-right half/half (session left; work,doc right). (2) mindmap_theme_light — solid blue top-level nodes with white bold uppercase, lighter blue children per depth with dark blue bold uppercase, inline refs lowercase not bold with box color font. Reordered mindmap source. Updated SKILL.md with layout instructions.

User shared GitHub rendered mindmap screenshot. Discovered mermaid mindmaps only support left-right layout (no top-down). Updated convention to left-right for both modes. Added mermaid theme init directive with light blue color scheme (primary #1565C0, secondary #42A5F5, tertiary #90CAF9).

User requested /mind-context call. Skill not registered in current session runtime. Executed manually — presented normal mode: dynamic nodes (session, work, documentation) + last 3 near_memory summaries. Need to investigate skill discovery.

Clarified platform differences: Claude Code (CLI, text-only) vs Claude Desktop (renders mermaid visually). User targets Claude Desktop via Project Knowledge approach. Confirmed light blue theme + left-right layout as the convention for Claude Desktop diagram output. Theme embedded in mind_memory.md via mermaid init directive.
User asked to see the mindmap. Output full mermaid source with blue theme for rendering.
